 {"id":"bd-bpe6","title":"Performance: stdio calls 4500x slower due to membrane overhead","notes":"puts() baseline: 31ns/call, with LD_PRELOAD: 138us/call (4500x slower). Root cause: each stdio call goes through runtime_policy::decide(), healing checks, scan_c_str_len(), and observe(). This causes smoke test performance failures (>2x threshold). Need fast-path bypass for hot stdio functions or optimized policy decision path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:45:22.050436442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.265027499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.262467997Z","close_reason":"Implemented strict/default stdio write fast path; rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed; focused assertions pass, with pre-existing stdio_abi_test teardown SIGSEGV tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1415,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Implemented strict/default fast path for hot stdio writes in stdio_abi.rs (fputc/fputs/fwrite/puts) so they bypass runtime_policy::decide/observe outside hardened mode while preserving hardened policy behavior. Formatted and diff-check clean; holding focused rch test until active peer builds clear.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:11:59Z"},{"id":1416,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Verification: rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed. Focused test strict_hot_stdio_writes_skip_runtime_policy_gate reported all assertions ok, but stdio_abi_test binary still exits SIGSEGV after teardown; existing puts_calls_without_crash and fputc_fgetc_and_ungetc_behave_consistently show the same ok-then-SIGSEGV pattern, so this is the pre-existing stdio ABI harness teardown crash rather than this fast-path assertion failing.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bpx9","title":"syscall: implement raw clone3","description":"Add a typed raw Linux clone3 syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core, plus focused syscall veneer tests covering invalid struct size and null-pointer error paths.  {"id":"bd-buup","title":"syscall: fsopen NULL fsname with nonzero flags should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"Host glibc returns EPERM for fsopen(NULL, 1) on this unprivileged host. Add focused ABI parity coverage so nonzero flags do not change the observed privilege-first result.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:13:23.773844248Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:13:23.773844248Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-bw0h","title":"resolv: add host-parity ABI probes for synchronous-only gai_* wrappers","description":"Concrete coverage gap: unistd_abi_test.rs currently hard-codes ENOSYS expectations for the getaddrinfo_a/gai_* wrapper family and does not fence the observed host-glibc degenerate semantics for null, empty, and zeroed-request inputs. Delivered alongside design (bd-15q). EWMA-smoothed 5-signal input, 5-arm Euler-weighted scoring, O(1) integer-only. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:40:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-by8c","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Runtime iconv dispatch integration with explicit fallback/error policy","description":"Background:
- Runtime codec selection must remain deterministic and safe even with partial codec coverage.

Goal:
- Integrate generated codec tables into runtime iconv path with explicit fallback and error policy.

Deliverables:
1) Runtime lookup/dispatch integration.
2) Fallback behavior for unsupported codecs.
3) Mode-aware safety handling and telemetry.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Supported conversions succeed deterministically.
- Unsupported conversions fail according to explicit contract.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for supported/unsupported conversion paths.
- Structured logs with trace_id, codec_pair, dispatch_path, outcome, errno.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletBasin","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.486299908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:53:30.710296662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T23:53:30.710097235Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","iconv","implementation","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-by8c","depends_on_id":"bd-13ya","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-by8c","depends_on_id":"bd-7cba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-byd9","title":"EPIC: Flat Combining for Contended State [Score 6.0, section 14.2]","description":"Implement flat combining for sequentially batching operations under contention. When multiple threads contend on shared state (e.g., global allocation statistics, arena rebalancing counters, evidence ledger writes), flat combining designates one thread as the 'combiner' that executes all pending operations sequentially, eliminating lock contention. How it works: (1) Each thread publishes its pending operation to a per-thread slot in a shared publication list. (2) One thread acquires the combiner lock. (3) The combiner scans all publication slots, collects pending operations, and executes them all sequentially. (4) The combiner writes results back to each thread's slot. (5) Other threads spin-wait on their slot until the result appears. Benefits: (a) Sequential execution eliminates all synchronization within the combined batch. (b) Cache locality — the combiner's working set stays hot. (c) Throughput scales better than lock-based approaches under high contention because the combiner amortizes lock acquisition cost across N operations. Target applications in FrankenLibC: global allocation statistics (malloc_stats/mallinfo), arena rebalancing decisions, evidence ledger batch writes, and TSM validation pipeline under burst load.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2 of graveyard (Score 6.0). Flat combining from Hendler, Hendler & Shavit (2010 SPAA). Publication list pattern for sequential batching under contention.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FlatCombiner<T, Op, R> generic implementation in src/concurrency/flat_combining.rs.
- First application: global allocation statistics (AllocStats).
- Future applications: arena rebalancing, evidence ledger batch writes, TSM pipeline burst handling.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
1. FlatCombiner generic implemented and applied to AllocStats.
2. Throughput >10x mutex at 32 threads for stat-heavy workloads.
3. No lost operations under any contention level.
4. Benchmark comparing flat combining vs mutex, RwLock, atomic counters.
5. Crossover point documented (thread count where flat combining wins).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Combiner activity at trace level. Benchmark results as JSON artifacts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:50.144746144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:41.325595364Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:41.325362824Z","close_reason":"Delivered or tracked as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1077,"issue_id":"bd-byd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:08Z"}]}
